explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The CEI violation in XferMgr.propExecute is real and exploitable via the same attack vector as the related Vault-level reentrancy finding. The execution cursor ph.iXfer is read into memory ctx.iXfer before the transfer loop, and written back to storage (ph.iXfer = iExec) only AFTER the loop completes (line 377). During ERC1155 safeTransferFrom or native coin xferNative, a reentrant call through Vault.execXferProp (which requires Agent role) back into XferMgr.propExecute reads ph.iXfer=0 (stale) and re-executes all transfers from the beginning. XferStatus.Sent is pre-initialized at proposal creation time (not an execution-time guard), and the loop only skips XferStatus.Skipped — Sent items are not idempotent and get processed again. The outer call then overwrites ph.iXfer with a smaller value than what the inner call stored, leaving the cursor corrupted. No reentrancy guard, per-pid mutex, or nonReentrant modifier exists anywhere in the call chain. The exploit requires a contract-based Agent that is also a transfer recipient — this combination is not prevented by the code and is consistent with the already-verified Vault-level reentrancy finding (fp_finding_02).
